name: env-paths
usage_summary: Single import in packages/cli-kit/src/private/node/constants.ts: `import envPaths from 'env-paths'`. Called twice: `envPaths(identifier).cache` for the cache folder (guarded by XDG_CACHE_HOME check) and `envPaths(identifier).log` for the logs folder. No subpath imports.
how_to_remove: env-paths resolves platform-specific directories: on macOS `~/Library/Caches/<name>` and `~/Library/Logs/<name>`, on Linux `~/.cache/<name>` and `~/.local/share/logs/<name>`, on Windows `%LOCALAPPDATA%/<name>/Cache` and `%LOCALAPPDATA%/<name>/Log`. A ~25-line inline function using `process.platform`, `os.homedir()`, and `process.env` (LOCALAPPDATA, XDG_CACHE_HOME, XDG_STATE_HOME) replaces it completely. Only two call sites need the `.cache` and `.log` properties.
replacement_reason: env-paths has had zero releases since January 2023 (last version 3.0.0) and is effectively unmaintained. The usage is limited to two property accesses (.cache, .log) for platform-specific directories. An inline implementation using os.homedir() and process.platform covers all three supported platforms with minimal code.
